design_principles: Fusion: Academic Rigor x Tropical Warmth
Content is King: Real language examples, no Lorem Ipsum
The Code-Phone: Build the device with CSS, not images
Swiss Grid: Alignment and typography drive the structure

typography.font_loading_instruction: Import fonts from Google Fonts in index.css: @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Serif+Display:ital@0;1&family=Manrope:wght@200;300;400;500;600;700;800&display=swap');

rules.subheadings: font-sans uppercase tracking-widest text-xs font-bold text-primary-600

colors.gradients: No full screen gradients. Use soft radial gradients for backgrounds: radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #B6C93220 0%, transparent 50%)

layout.grid_system: Use a 12-column grid for the main container. Marketing sections should use 'The Tetris Grid' (Mode A).
layout.spacing: Relaxed. Use p-8 to p-24 for section padding.

Hero.name: The Polyglot Split
Hero.structure: Split screen. Left: Typography + CTA. Right: The Code-Phone.
Hero.visuals: Background: #F9F9F7 with subtle noise texture. Foreground: Large serif headline 'Hable el mundo'.
Hero.interaction: The Code-Phone on the right should float (animate-float). Floating 'Chat Bubbles' with greetings in 5 languages surround it.

CodePhone.classes: relative border-8 border-gray-900 rounded-[2.5rem] h-[600px] w-[300px] bg-white shadow-2xl overflow-hidden
CodePhone.content: Inside the phone, render the 'Lesson Preview' component.

LanguageSelector.name: The Ticket Rail
LanguageSelector.item_style: Card appearing like a travel ticket or passport stamp. White bg, dashed border, flag icon, language name.

LevelRoadmap.name: The Metro Line
LevelRoadmap.visuals: A continuous vertical line connecting circles (stations) labeled A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2.
LevelRoadmap.interaction: Clicking a station expands the details (Accordion style). Active station glows.

LessonPreview.name: The Live Demo
LessonPreview.structure: Tabs (Vocab, Grammar, Chat) inside the Code-Phone.
LessonPreview.content: Real data only. No Lorem Ipsum. Use the provided Content Strategy data.

Flashcards.name: The Flip Deck
Flashcards.interaction: Click to flip (CSS transform: rotateY(180deg)). Buttons for 'Know it' (Green) / 'Study again' (Orange).

FeatureGrid.name: Bento Features
FeatureGrid.structure: Grid of 3 items. 1 Large (AI Chat), 2 Small (TTS, Quizzes).
FeatureGrid.style: Bento grid style. Rounded corners, soft background colors.

levels.title: Acceso (A1)
levels.desc: Principiante. Entiende y usa expresiones cotidianas.

levels.title: Plataforma (A2)
levels.desc: Básico. Se comunica en tareas sencillas y habituales.

levels.title: Umbral (B1)
levels.desc: Intermedio. Lidiar con situaciones de viaje.

levels.title: Avanzado (B2)
levels.desc: Intermedio Alto. Entiende textos complejos y técnicos.

levels.title: Dominio (C1)
levels.desc: Avanzado. Se expresa con fluidez y espontaneidad.

levels.title: Maestría (C2)
levels.desc: Experto. Comprende todo lo que oye y lee.

grammar.title: Verbo Gustar
grammar.rule: A mí me gusta la playa. (I like the beach)

assets.icons: Use lucide-react. Examples: MessageCircle (Chat), Mic (TTS), Brain (AI), BookOpen (Lessons), Plane (Travel/Languages).

images.hero_bg: Use a subtle CSS pattern. DO NOT use stock photos if specific ones aren't provided. Use 'noise' texture.
images.flags: Use standard Emoji flags for reliability.

universal_guidelines: Do not use generic 'Inter' font for headings. Use 'DM Serif Display'.
Do not use full-screen gradients. Use subtle blobs.
Do not center everything. Use split layouts.
